description: Kenya Airways has negative equity of approximately KES 6 billion and total debt exceeding KES 40 billion. The airline's financial restructuring plan requires sustained government support and successful debt renegotiation with lenders.
mitigationPlan: Implement financial restructuring plan; negotiate debt-to-equity conversion with lenders; pursue strategic equity partnership; reduce operating costs through fleet optimisation.

description: Intense competition from Ethiopian Airlines, Emirates, and low-cost carriers on key routes is eroding Kenya Airways market share. The airline's hub strategy at JKIA faces competitive pressure from Addis Ababa as the preferred East African hub.
mitigationPlan: Strengthen JKIA hub position; expand African network; develop code-share partnerships; improve on-time performance and customer experience; pursue fleet renewal for fuel efficiency.

description: Kenya Power's high system losses of approximately 23% (against a target of 18%) result in significant revenue leakage. Combined with high cost of power purchase from IPPs, the utility faces margin compression and tariff adequacy challenges.
mitigationPlan: Implement smart metering rollout to reduce commercial losses; upgrade distribution network to reduce technical losses; renegotiate IPP power purchase agreements; pursue EPRA tariff review.

description: Aging distribution infrastructure in Nairobi and secondary cities results in frequent outages and high maintenance costs. Average outage duration (SAIDI) of 28 hours per customer per year significantly exceeds the EPRA target of 15 hours.
mitigationPlan: Prioritise distribution network rehabilitation in high-density areas; implement predictive maintenance programme; deploy automated fault detection systems.

description: Competition from the Port of Dar es Salaam and Djibouti for landlocked country cargo (Uganda, Rwanda, DRC) poses a risk to KPA's regional market share. Tanzania's SGR investment is improving Dar es Salaam's competitiveness.
mitigationPlan: Improve port efficiency and turnaround times; reduce port charges for transit cargo; enhance SGR integration for inland container depot connectivity; develop Lamu Port as alternative gateway.

description: Tax evasion and illicit financial flows remain significant challenges, with an estimated KES 300 billion annual tax gap. Informal sector under-taxation and transfer pricing abuse by multinationals are key contributors.
mitigationPlan: Expand iTax digital platform to informal sector; strengthen transfer pricing audit capacity; implement beneficial ownership registry; enhance data analytics for risk-based audit selection.

description: Implementation of the NSSF Act 2023 increasing mandatory contributions has faced legal challenges and employer resistance. Uncertainty over the Act's implementation timeline creates risk of contribution shortfalls and fund growth targets not being met.
mitigationPlan: Engage stakeholders on NSSF Act implementation; pursue legal clarity through courts; develop phased implementation plan; enhance member communication and education.

description: KDB's loan portfolio concentration in SME and agribusiness sectors creates vulnerability to climate-related shocks and commodity price volatility. NPL ratio of 12% exceeds the CBK benchmark of 8%.
mitigationPlan: Diversify loan portfolio across sectors; strengthen credit appraisal for climate-sensitive sectors; increase loan loss provisions; implement early warning system for emerging NPLs.
